translation_text: [en] 25th regnal year, Hathyr of Pharaoh Ptolemaios (III.) and of the Female Pharaoh Berenike (II.) 25th regnal year, Hathyr of Ptolemaios (III.), son of Ptolemaios (II.), and of the Female Pharaoh Berenike (II), the gods Euergetai. Pinyris, son of Estphenis is the one who says to Milon, the attorney of Euphronios praktor: ‘ By Pharaoh Ptolemaios (III.) and the Female Pharaoh Berenike (II.) and the gods Adelphoi, who created them, and the gods Soteres, who created those who created them, and the gotes Euergetai and Isis and Osiris-Apis and the other gods: The half of the field in the land section(?)of Ta-set-taiu- -Neith, which amounts to 1 aroura of field(?), which gave away Euphronios praktor, against repayment of my mortgage. The same field: It formerly belongs to Estphenis, my father. He made him over to Tathres, my mother. He had no master besides me till the giving-away-of-it, which Euphronios executed, against repayment. It may live my face!’ Issued by Pinyris, son of Estphenis, in the 25th regnal year, Hathyr of Pharaoh Ptolemaios (III.), son of Ptolemaios (II.), and of the Female Pharaoh Berenike (II.) {and the gods Adelphoi} {and} the gods Euergetai. Countersigned by Pinyris, son of Estphenis, in accordance with that which was written above.

title: Ostr. Berlin P. 12263
transcription_text: Tꜣ-šr.t-n-Hy Tꜣ-šr.t-n-Pꜣ- Pꜣ-šr-n-pꜣ-ḥ.t(?) Ns-mt(r) For this short form see E. Lüddeckens et al., Demotisches Namenbuch, Wiesbaden 1980-2000, 664 – 666 nos. 46, 48, 53 – 60. The second part mt was written wit the Demotic numeral “10”, Coptic Ⲙⲏⲧ. pꜣ ꜥꜣ sꜣ Pa-H̱nm sꜣ ꜥrꜥ Krḏ pꜣ sn n Ḥr-m-ḥb Tꜣ-šr.t-ta-Tꜣ-šr.t-n-Pꜣ-šr-Hry=w ḥḏ dbn For the Roman form of ḥḏ cf. also Wångstedt, in OrSu 31-32 (1982-83), 25 Kat. XV. 5 Pꜣ-šr-n-Pa-H̱nm (sꜣ) Prgtrw This should be a Greek male name whose interpretation is unclear. Thus a name was not mentioned in E. Lüddeckens et al., Demotisches Namenbuch, Wiesbaden 1980-2000. ḥḏ dbn For the Roman form of ḥḏ cf. also Wångstedt, in OrSu 31-32 (1982-83), 25 Kat. XV. 5 Rnp.t-nfr.t sꜣ ꜣgn Thus a name was not mentioned in E. Lüddeckens et al., Demotisches Namenbuch, Wiesbaden 1980-2000, but presumably this is a Demotic variant of the (rarely attested) name Ἀγενις / Agenis (Trismegistos name id 1776). Cf. also the late Ptolemaic name ꜥgn (KAPLONY-HECKEL, URSULA, 1963. Die demotischen Tempeleide. Ägyptologische Abhandlungen 6, Wiesbaden, no. 125:2). ḥḏ dbn For the Roman form of ḥḏ cf. also Wångstedt, in OrSu 31-32 (1982-83), 25 Kat. XV. 5 This line is entirely enigmatic. One would normally expect a total in the form like r qd 5 “yields 5 kite” or something similar. The first three lines may be read as r sttr 1.t, but this is definitely much too less as being the total of the above listed payments. The last two signs remain without any explanation. For comments on this part I am indebted to the participiants of the Demotic Summer School 2018 in Munich.

transcription_text: Pꜣ-ḏı͗-ꜣs.t-r-ḥꜣ.t This seems to be a short form for ꜣs.t-ḥꜣt. pꜣ-mšꜥ „Isis in front o the army”, a local form of Isis attested in Aswan: LÜDDECKENS, Demotisches Namenbuch, 293. [_] tgm | Probqnly r-bnr r. This phrase, also present here in line 6 and on Ostr. Oxf. Ashm 797 and Ostr.BM EA 12090, remains unclear. 1.t Tꜣ-šr.t-n-Wsı͗r(?) tgm rtb 6 Pꜣ-ḏı͗-Wsı͗r-ns-mt(r) For this short form see Lüddeckens, Demotisches Namenbuch (1979-2000), 664 – 666 nos. 46, 48, 53 – 60. The second part mt was written wit the Demotic numeral “10”, Coptic Ⲙⲏⲧ. sꜣ Pꜣ-šr-Mn [_] rtb 2 mḏ(ꜣ.t) 6 The numeral behind mḏꜣ.t is unusual in the previous interpretation that mḏꜣ.t is only a measure for wheat (= 1/12 artabas). Nur el-Din, O. Leiden dem, cat. 158 published a similar form. He suggested, that a part of the wheat was measured in mḏꜣ.t, another part of the wheat in another measure. See also Mattha, O. Mattha, cat 267 - 268. Here it is clear, that is meant: mḏꜣ.t-unity x. Lwpꜥ This name is currently not known to me elsewhere. (sꜣ) Pꜣ-ḫṱ(?) sꜣ Wsı͗r-ı͗w(?) ..?.. title: Ostr. Berlin P. 12916
transcription_text: Ḥr-pa-ꜣs.t For information on the writings of the amounts of money I am indebted to S. Lippert, Montpellier.sꜣ Hrı͗=w Names with this form of writing are also being interpreted as Hrj-Ptḥ, cf. Lüddeckens, Demotisches Namenbuch (1979-2000), 746 – 748, 772. ḥḏ dbn For a discussion on the reading of such signs I am indebted to BRIAN MUHS, Chicago. For Roman forms of ḥḏ cf. Wångstedt in OrSu 31-32 (1982-83), 25 Kat. XV. 10 H̱nm-ı͗w 7-ı͗rm-½ Transcription and translation according to S. Lippert (paper presented on the 13th ICDS in Leipzig 2017), this part must be in obols (not mentioned) and represents the equivalent to the amount of 1 silver drachma = ½ silver kite.Pꜣ-šr-Pꜣ-Swrs This name seems to be a combination of an Egyptian with a Greek one and is otherwise not known to me. For the Greek part cf. the currently only once attested name Swr ( Lüddeckens, Demotisches Namenbuch (1979-2000), 909.), which can be a transliteration of a Greek name Σούλις/ Σῦλις (The form Σουλιος seems to be a nominative. Cf. also the once attested female name Σουλωις: Clarysse / Thompson, P. Count (2006), 32,37, from Early Ptolemaic Arsinoites.). Once attested from the cemetery of Sohag is the female name Σενψούλις from the 4th Century CE (SB 10 10501,2) , which is given in Trismegistos as deriving from “Tꜣ-šr.t-n-..?..”. The – currently unattested – male equivalent to this female name should be Psenpsoulis/*Πσενπσούλις, cf. the Name Πσούλις with its several varying Greek writing forms. This will fit well the name attested on the ostracon here. Πσούλις is transferred by the Egyptian scribe in Pꜣ-Swrs, possibly in unawareness of the original nature of the name. According to this, we can reconstruct the female name from Sohag as being Tꜣ-šr.t(-n)-Pꜣ-Swrs. (ḥḏ) qd ½ Curiously, the group for the currency kite is written behind the cipher. This can be found on some other Elephantine accounts and should be a local variant, possibly deriving from gs qd = “half of a kite”. It is remarkable that the same amount in l. 2 and 3 is written in a different way, but due to the information of S. LIPPERT from her material from Hut-Repit that there was a rule that sums lower than 5 stater were given in stater, but in contrast sums higher than 5 stater were written in deben+kite. ḥḏ dbn 2½ dmḏ (ḥḏ) qd 1 ḥḏ dbn 12 ½ It is unusual that the smaller amount kite is noted before the higher amount of the deben and not behind as usual. This can be found also on Ostr. Berlin P. 12954 (stater before deben), seems to be a local variant in Roman Elephantine.

title: Ostr. Berlin P. 12974
transcription_text: qtme=f qtme=k qtme=k qtme=f stf=f For this word, if correctly identified, cf. CDD_s, 512-513, ERICHSEN, Demotisches Glossar, 478. stf=k stf =k stf=f bs=f This word may be connected with bs “”a metal vessel”, cf. ERICHSEN, Demotisches Glossar, 122, and CDD_B, 82. This determinative written here does not appear in any of the known attestations of this word, but all other words with bs seem to fitting here well. Maybe the determinative is a scribal error bs=k bs=k bs=f ḥmꜣ=f ḥmꜣ=k ḥmꜣ=k ḥmꜣ=f /// The wiped out parts were written in front of nearly all wrr-writings. Τ]α̣νεσε̣[ -?- ἀπὸ Ἐλεφαντίνης ὡς (ἐτῶν) μα, μ -ca.?- μετὰ κυρίου Παχομπαοννώφρεος Φα̣ ̣ ̣ ̣ ἱερέως Ἄμ- μωνος θεου̂ μεγίστου Παχομπετενεφώτῃ Ὀννώφρεως μητρὸς Τισάτις ἱερεῖ Ἴσιδος θε̣- ᾶς μεγίστης ἐπίτροπον πεποιηκέναι α(ὐτὸν) ἐπὶ τῷ πωλῆσαι τὸν ὑπάρχοντα αὐτῇ οἰκογενηι l. οἰ[κογεν]ῆ δου̂λον, ᾧ ὄνομα Νάρκισσος, ὡς (ἐτῶν) η, οὐλὴ χειρὶ δεξιᾷ ἐκ τῆς ὑπαρχούσης αὐτῇ καὶ με̣τηλλαχυ-ειης l. με[τηλλαχ]υ|ίας δούλης Ἀφροδείτης -?- , ἐφʼ ᾧ πωλήσει ὁ Παχομπετενεφώτης τὸν δοῦλον Νάρκισσον καὶ ἀποκαταστήσει τὴν τούτου τειμὴν l. τιμὴν τῇ ἑαυτου̂ πίστει τῇ προγεγραμμένῃ Ταουερσηου̂τι ἐκ πλή- ρους, ἐξέσται δὲ αὐτῷ οἰκονομεῖν π[ερὶ] τὸν δηλούμενον δ̣ου̂λον, ὡς ἐὰν αἱρηται καὶ ἣν ἐὰν ποιήσηται περὶ τούτου ἀσφάλειαν κυρία̣ν εἶναι ὡς καὶ ἑαυτῆς παρούσης. Transcription and translation of the Demotic by Jan Moje. I am indebted to Andreas Winkler for discussing this text in the context of other slave contracts. Ta-Wrše-ꜥꜣ.t ta Wn-nfr nty rmṯ.(.t) Such full writings of rmṯ are attested in Roman times: ERICHSEN, Demotisches Glossar, 247. m Yb nty ḏd n Pa-H̱nm-Pꜣ-ḏı͗-Nfr-ḥtp sꜣ Wn-nfr mw.t=f Tꜣy-Sty tw=y ı͗r n.ı͗m=k ⲙ̅ⲙⲟⲕ. n ꜣpytrpws The Greek title ἐπίτροπος from the first part of the papyrus is transliterated into Demotic, which seems to be the first attestation in Demotic. For the Greek term see Straus, La terminologie de l'esclavage (reference due to A. WINKLER). r-dbꜣ pꜣ šr ẖr.t nty ḏd n=f Nꜥrgss nty pꜣy=f ꜥḥꜥ n ꜥnḫ rnp.t 8.t r-dbꜣ-ḥḏ n(?) mtw=k ı͗rm=f ı͗w wn mtw=k s wn md(.t) nb dy r.r=f r-ẖ.t pꜣ nty ı͗w=w r-šn=k n.ı͗m=f nty r dbꜣ-ḥḏ n nꜣ nty ı͗r mrẖ(?) twy=s Pa-H̱nm sꜣ Wn-nfr sꜣ Pa-/// In the Greek part, the father of Pachompaonnophis is mentioned as Φα[///], the rest is unfortuantely lost in both languages. Due to the small lacuna in the Demotic part, the name must have been rather short. I assume Pa-wn (Phagonis), Pꜣ-ḥtr (Phates), or Pa-Mn (Phaminis), Pa-ꜥw (Paous). pꜣy=s ṯs pꜣ ḥm-nṯr I͗mn r ḫrw=s (ı͗w⸗f) ḫpr (ı͗w) bw-ı͗r⸗y gꜣ(?) ı͗w=f ı͗r yꜥyb.w Writing for yꜥby, which regularly means “illness”. Here the final sign looks like the divine determinative, which may point to epilepsy which is called in antiquity as the holy, divine illness, mentioned in Greek slave contracts as ἳερος νόσος, cf. URBANIK, Slave defects, passim. Due to this, I tentatively translate “epilepsy” here, r nty-ḥrj n-ḏr.t n ḥsb.t 20.t n 𓍹ꜣwṱgrtr ꜥws 𓍹Gysrs 𓍹Nrwys This element does not appear in the Greek sequence of the imperator’s identification, but due to the available space I assume this name here. Only for Gysrs the space would be too large, besides the line-break within the word would be rather unusual. Therefore, nothing else but „Nerva” might fit in the lacuna. 𓍹Trꜥyꜥns ꜥws 𓍹ꜣrystw Sbsṱs 𓍹Grmꜥnyks Tꜥgygꜥws Prtrygws ı͗bd 4 ꜣḫ.t sw 11 (trace, but no text)
translation_text: [en] In year 20 of Imperator Caesar Trajan Optimus Augustus Germanicus Dacicus Parthicus, Choiak 11? in --- of the Thebaid before the notaries NN, son of Ammonios and NN, son of Gaius. Thus acknowledges Taouerseous, daughter of Onnophris and the mother Senesentis?, one of those fromElephantine, 41 years old, ---, with her guardian Pachompaonnophris, son of Pha---, priest of Ammon the great god, to Pachompetenephotes, son of Onnophris and the mother Tisatis, priest of Isis?, thethe great godess, to having appointed him as mandatary forthe purpose of selling the houseborn slave belonging to her,whose name is Narkissos, 8 years old, with a mark on theright hand, born out of her own deceased female slave Aphrodite, on the condition that Pachompetenephotes shall sell the slave Narkissos and shall hand over the price for him, acting on his own good faith, to the aforesaid Taouerseous in full.It shall be permitted to him to proceed with regard to the aforesaid slave however he chooses, and the contract whichever he will draw up concerning him (the slave) shall be valid as if she herself was present. Taorseos, daughter of Onnophris, who is a woman from [Elephantine, is the one who speaks to Pa]chnoumis-Petenephotes, son of Onnophris and of his mother Tisatis: ‘I make you the authority regarding the son of [my(?)] female slave [///] [///], who is called Narkissos, [8 years old], ⸢..?..⸣ against payment will belong to you [///]⸢..?..⸣ ⸢..?..⸣[///] in that he belongs to you. And if there is anything here against him, according to that, what you will be asked about him ⸢..?.⸣ ⸢..?..⸣ [who against pay]ment from those who carry on a lawsuit since(?) [///] Pachnoumis, son of Onnophris, [son of Pha///], her [guardian, the] priest of Amun, on her order, if I will not [/// or] if he suffers from epilepsy [///] ⸢..?..⸣[///], which was mentioned above. Issued in the 20th regnal year of Imperator LPH Caesar Nerva Traianus LPH Optimus Augustus Germanicus Dacicus Parthicus, 11th of Choiak. (trace, but no text)

translation_text: [en] [One may inquire] (my) fortune before Isis as follows: Is it (my) good fortune to travel to the nome at the day of receiving silver money for grain? Or is it better to stay here on Elephantine? The good fortune: One knows For the translation cf. Den Brinker / Muhs / Vleeming, DBL (2005), 54. it through you. It is a matter which should not decay. One may inquire (the oracle) to be safe! Issued (in) the 2nd regnal year, 29th of Mecheir.

translation_text: [en] Gemstones(?) which will be huge (i.e measured?) by their estimate. From Horos, son of Pasenis: That which has given to him Harnouphis, son of Chnoum-neb-nai-seanch: 1st sac: By estimate 3 (deben). From Harnouphis, son of Chnoum-(neb)-nai-seanch: 2nd sac: By estimate 5 (deben), 7 kite. Specification: One (sac), by estimate 7 kite. That what was delivered into the temple of Thebes: One (sac), by estimate 5 (deben) in total. They were sealed all by my (own) hand. They may be brought into the house of gold! Take care of the people working (with the gemstones)! Because of the genuine lapis lazuli I caused them to be sent to Memphis. Written by Teos, son of Nepherpres, in the 6th regnal year, 21st of Phamenoth.

translation_text: [en] against me. And I will give [///] installments(?) [///] 5th regnal year, Mesore: 1 silver (deben); 6th regnal year, Paophi: 1 silver (deben); Tybi: 1 silver (deben). Yields 3 silver (deben) again. The amount of money, which I will not give at the [above mentioned] dates, this [I will] give together with one and a half times its value within that month which is after the month mentioned, without any objection. And I will [cause,] that 10 aruras farmland will be plowed for you from the offering donation of Chnoum anew, as they are plowed and (equipped) constantly with cattle (and) grain. The farmland thereof which (I) will not restitute to you from these 10 aruras farmland mentioned above: I will deliver its crop disposal to you: 15 (artabas) emmer per 1 (arura) field. And you will pay the crop disposal of Chnoum from them, with necessity and without delay or objection. Issued by Sochotes, son of Chons-pa-meter in the 5th regnal year, 25th of Epeiph. Countersigned Pachnoumis, son of Teos. Countersigned Nechtminis, son of Horos. Countersigned Panouphis, son of Artais. Countersigned by Onnophris, son of Sochotes.

translation_text: [en] [Voice of NN before NN,] son of Eschnoumpmetis, and the scribes of Chnoum: May one give a goose, yields the 12th part of a calf, yields a goose again, to the attorney of Amyntas, the assistant of Pachmounis, who came to the south to inspect Elephantine (and) Syene! Do not deny him this! Written by Sehapmoys in the 5th regnal year, 2nd of Pachons. I will deliver his compensation to the south or his value in an amount of money. Do not delay anything!

translation_text: [en] They have spoken [///] One is used to quarrel with him concerning a matter. Petemous does not belong to those people whose hands (can) be taken in a matter. There is a letter made by the wab priests of … to the of … . One knows the strong one, who arose(?). I could not cause a wab priest to come to the south. The wab priests of Osiris who are (also) priests of Chnoum and who (live) here, inquired about him. They agreed. They wrote a letter to the wab priests, too. The lesonis may be ordered to cause me to hear about the suspension which is to be granted to Petemous because of Peteesis, his son. And it should be caused, that Petemous send to me concerning everything which shall be done to him. If it happens that here is something (which is desired): One may order to cause to send to me concerning it! Written by Teonurisephonychos, son of Imouthes, in the 9th regnal year, 4th of Payni.

translation_text: [en] [///] of sailing which Hakoris has made. I have sent to you concerning this. There is no bad saying(?) without assistance(?) by(?) Horos. Chnoum has not caused them to attain to his hand. I have found that he went to Philae. He has spoken many words to Tegeritli-ye concerning my case. To his statement, he did not accept any word of my case. I have come to the temple of Elephantine with my men. I am at the resting-place of Chnoum. The things for which the fodderer Amasis came hither: You heave heard them, (but) you have not sent to me on account of that which has happened to you. There is no (harm) to the young men who are (in) Thebes, and to the men who are there. Osoroeris, son of Artais: When he comes to the south, let it be ordered to look after him. Written in the 19th regnal year, 5th of Hathyr. If you meet with the overseer of the field, let him know the matters which have happened to me.

translation_text: [en] I perform the blessings of Eschnompmetis, the high priest, before Osiris, Horus, Isis (and) the gods of Abydos NOTE. They may grant that you are exalted among the praised ones of Chnoum, the great god. Thus has sent to me Parates (A) NOTE, son of Payris concerning the 4 5/6 silver kite: ‘Thus has spoken Parates (B), son of Inaros: I have given them (scil. the money) to Parates (C), son of Eskasothes, (in) your name. There is no money which Parates (B), son of Inaros, would have given to me (in) the name of Parates (A), son of Payris. There is no money which he would have given (to me), precisely nothing! Osiris, the great god, may cause me to stand before the high priest without blemish. Written in the 24th regnal year, 6th of Pachons.

translation_text: [en] The oath which has taken Thotomous, son of Harpaesis, to Euphronios, praktor of the temples, in 23rd regnal year, 17th of Phamenoth. The priest of (the god) Horus-son-of-Isis, priest of Ptah, priest of the Gods Euergetai Thotomous, son of Harpaesis, is the one who speaks to Euphronios, praktor [of the temples]: [I take an oath] in the name of Pharaoh Ptolemaios (III.), son of Ptolemaios (II.), and (in the name of) the Female Pharaoh Berenike (II.) and (in the name of) the gods Adelphoi, (and in the name of) the gods Euergetai and of Isis [and Serapis and] of all [gods] of Egypt. I will hold a meeting in the nome of Edfu, together with your attorneys. The arrears, which I have recorded for you in those years in which I should have collected them, according to that was was written for you by the scribes (of) [your] attorneys, those I will hand over to you together with the interest burdened(?) on them. Provided that I shall take the oath above as a true oath, I will be among those who are favored by Pharaoh. If I will take it as a false one, I will be in punishment, and I will not escape to a place-of-oath (or) a place-of-protection. Written in 23rd regnal year, 17th of Phamenoth. Oath of Thotomous, son of Harpaesis, Hierogrammateus from Edfou concerning the obligations, so that he fulfills them accordingly.

translation_text: [en] 23rd regnal year, Phamenoth, of Pharaoh Ptolemaios (III.), son of Ptolemaios (II.), and of the Female Pharaoh Berenike (II.), { the gods Adelphoi,} the gods Euergetai. Harpaesis, son of Peteharsomtous is the one who speaks: I take the oath to Pharaoh mentioned above: You scribes, who are responsible for the royal linen, and you scribes of Horus of Edfou, (and) of the gods Adelphoi (and) the gods Euergetai: I will give it apart from the ¼ - beyond the place- -of-oath and the place-of-protection. Harsiesis, son of Peteharsomtus, the inspector of the temple in Edfou [is the one who speaks to] [the attorney Euph]ronios, praktor of the temple in the district of [Edfou]. I take an oath in the name of Pharaoh Ptolemaios (III.), [son of] Ptolemaios (II.), and of the Female Pharaoh Berenike (II.), of the gods Adelphoi, of the Gods Euergetai, and of Isis and Osiris and of all gods of Egypt: Those people, who have been high priest in the temple of Edfou, who owe arrears for the royal linen, I will give it(?) in the hand(?) of --?-- and of your attorney beyond the place-of-oath and the place-of-protection. I will let the scribes of the temple sit with your representative, [so that they control] the arrears, in order to note them. I shall take care of the arrears [///], as I will not delay them (scil. their payment). That (amount), which the scribes of the temples will not [declare] for everyone, by prescribing him for payment, with this I shall provide your representative, as the wine(?) of the temple will be registered, which I will not let be made ..?.. mentioned above(?) --?—If I will take the oath as a true oath, I shall be among among those who are favored by Pharaoh, If I will take it as a false one, I shall be punished Instead of rm „Tränen“ (Spiegelberg), cf. Den Brinker / Muhs / Vleeming, DBL (2005), 202.. [///] Harsiesis, (son of) Peteharsomtus. He says: I will act according to every word mentioned above. 23rd regnal year, 20th of Phamenoth. The oath of Harsieses, the representative of the epistates Skonometis, the priest in Edfou under the (following) condition: The debtors of the linen [///] he will assign to Andron, (furthermore:) The temple arrears, he will produce them.

translation_text: [en] To Milon, praktor of the temples. A memorandum from Pinyris, son of Estphenis, and Psintaes Junior, son of Estphenis. a) Considering that it happened that our one field was given (away) against repayment, which is situated in Psebtomit in the nome of Edfou, (and) which represents 30 aruras of field, because our payment was agreed for giving concerning Estphenis, son of Pinyris, for the rest, which became his burden concerning byssus and the temple disposal of the 23rd regnal year [and (concerning)] Berenebthis, son of Estphenis for the same value of byssus of the 2nd and 19th regnal year, also his rest; b) (and considering that it happened) [that] it (scil. the field) was given away against repayment, in order to complete 16 silver (deben): Thus we may request from you: Considering that it happened [that] we have paid our amounts of money together with their interest rate with cash to the bank of Pharaoh within 60 days according to the order of Pharaoh, so it is fine, if you may do (the following): Compose in our presence a letter of fixtures and fittings in order that it may happen. If that will happen, than we shall not have been damaged. Issued by Estphenis, son of Pinyris. 25th regnal year, 19th of Paophi. Countersigned by Pinyris, son of Estph[enis]. Countersigned by Berenebthis, son of Estphenis. [Countersigned by Psintaes, son] of Es- -tphenis.

translation_text: [en] The scribes of Horus of Edfou, of the gods Adelphoi (and) of the gods Euergetai are the ones who speak: [_] Register of the shares, which are accorded the wab priests of Horus of Edfou due to the writing below, of the temple of Edfou. Thotomous junior, son of Peteharsomtus, the lesonis of the 15th year: 1½ (temple) years of the temple of Edfou. Peteharpochrates, son of Petamounis, the lesonis of the 10th year and the 20th year: ⅓ of a (temple) year of the temple of Edfou. Berenebthis, (son of) Estphenis, the lesonis of the 2nd year (and) the 7th year; [_] The share of the priest of Hathor --?--, whose ⅓ of one year have been awarded(?) to Hathor of Edfou. Peteharpochrates, son of Thotomous, the lesonis of the 22nd year: The half of one (temple) year [of the temple of Edfou]. Thotomous senior, son of Harsiesis, the lesonis of the 18th year: He has no income. Issued in the 24th regnal year, [///] of Phaophi. Issued by Psenchnoumis, son of [///]. Countersigned by Parates, son of Esminis.

translation_text: [en] (Memorandum) To Milon, the agent ofEuphronios and commissioner of the temples, from Pinyris, son of Estphenis, andPsintaes the younger, son of Estphenis. Since our land which is situated inPsebtomit of the Apollonopolites,in the size of 30 arouras, was forfeited as security, which we have put up for Estphenis, son of Pinyris, as security for the debthe owed for thetax on linen production and the incomeof the temple of the year 23, likewise for what Berenebthis owed towards the sametax on linen production of year 19 and year 2, 320 silver drachmae,we ask you, since in thedays allowed by the ordinancewe have paid the silverwith interest to the royalbank, please, give us (a document of ) release.When this happens, we will nothave suffered injustice.Farewell. Year 25 Phaophi 19.Countersigned by Pinyris, son of Estphenis. Countersigned by Berenebthis, son of Estphenis. Countersigned by Psintaes, son of Estphenis.

translation_text: [en] Nepherpremachis greets Es[chnoum]pmetis, the [le]sonis. Oh may Pre cause his lifetime to be long! You have satisfied (my) heart with the ¼ of the money, which you have transcribed to Inchy(?), in order to give them to me (by Inchy). You have given them to me (by yourself). (My) heart is satisfied with them. (I) will cause Inchy(?) to withdraw from you with respect to it. If (I) do not cause him to withdraw from you, (I) will endow you with 5 silver (deben), without taking any lawsuit on earth against you. Issued by Nepherpremachis, son of Parates, in the 30th regnal year, 16th of Mecheir.Artais, son of Benpouchnoumchaaroys, Esbendetis, son of [Har]yothes, Espmetsepsis, the fourth priest, Osoroeris, son of Petenouphis.

translation_text: [en] Eschnompmetis, son of Snebonychos is the one who speaks to Herakleid(es), the (chief) Lit.„He of the southern land“, see also l. 8 of this papyrus. of the southern land: If it is pleasing to you and if you organize my case with the chief of Thebes, and if you cause me to be appointed as lesonis of Chnoum, for 20 silver (deben), concerning which I have already sent to the chief of Thebes, then I will give 5 silver (deben), yields 25 staters, yields 5 silver (deben) again, to the house of the (chief) of the Southern land. Their payment date Lit.: „Time of giving“.: 3rd regnal year, Epeiph: 1 silver (deben), 5 kite. 4th regnal year, Phaophi: 1 silver (deben). Tybi: 1 (deben). Pharmuthi: 1 silver (deben), 5 kite. Yields (total) 5 silver (deben) again. Written by Eschnompmetis, son of Snebonychos, in the 3rd regnal year, 29th of Payni.

translation_text: [en] Peteharpochrates, son of Esyris. May one djepet-jar of wine be given to Eschnompmetis, the lesonis. We will give you [[his]] its NOTE exchange in the 2nd regnal year, Pachons. If we do not give it to you in the 2nd regnal year, Pachons, we shall give you 2 silver kite, Yields 1 stater, yields 2 kite again, for it in the 2nd regnal year, Payni with necessity and without delay. Issued by Psenchonsis, (son) of Esonnophris, in the 2nd regnal year, 7th of Hathyr, of Pharaoh Ptolemaios LHP Countersigned by Espmetis, son of Chnoumemes.

translation_text: [en] The memorandum. Causing to send me behind the army within two days to the Southern Land, or causing to send [someone else?] [///], to be there(?) . Suggestion made by Zauzich, cited after Thesaurus Linguae Aegyptiae. Because of hunger in which I am. Horos, the slave of Pasis, to cause to come for him. Phibis, son of Thoteus, the man from Elkab. Teteimouthes, the slave of Thanouphis. The garden area, which is located in Syene. The slave. By Pamonasis.

Den Brinker / Muhs / Vleeming, DBL (2005), 25. nty ḥry sẖ Ḥr-pa-ꜣs.t In line 1 and 14 the name of this person is given as Harpaesis. sꜣ Pꜣ-wp.ṱ ḏd ı͗w=y (r) ı͗r r-ẖ mḏ(.t) nb nty sẖ ḥry n nꜣ ssw.w nty ḥry 2nd copy Ḥr-pa-ꜣs.t sꜣ Pꜣ-wp.ṱ Ns-pꜣ-mtr sꜣ Ḏḥwtı͗-mꜣꜥ Pa-rṱ sꜣ Pa-ḥr r s 3 nꜣ nty ḏd (n) wꜥ rꜣ tw=n
translation_text: [en] Harpaesis, son of Pouoptis, Espmetis, son of Thotomous, (and) Parates, son of Payris – yields 3 people – are the one who say unanimously: We take an oath before Pharaoh Ptolemaios (III.), son of Ptolemaios (II.) and Arsinoë (II.) Philadelpha, {and} the gods Adelphoi, and the gods Euergetai, {and} the Female Pharaoh Berenike (II.), his sister, his wife {and the gods Adelphoi, the gods Euergetai} and Isis and Osiris and all the gods of Egypt. We will collect the amounts of money of the salt tax and the amounts of money of the wool tax of the district of Elephantine, for the tax of the 11th regnal year, the 3 people in accordance with that which will be accounted for us in the houses of writing of Pharaoh, without any men saying something wrong to his companion among us, the 3 people. And they shall be paid to the banks of Pharaoh until we have paid them in full. And we will bear the allowance and the loss between us, the 3 people. That which shall arise as profit, we will divide it among us, the 3 people, so that there is no falsehood n the oath. And we will write the money which shall be paid to us, the 3 people, `and´ the expense which shall arise therein, `in a papyrus roll and we will seal it´. The one who will withdraw from it according with everything mentioned above, he should pay 5 silver (deben) (to) his companion among us. Issued by Parates, son of Payris, on declaration of the three men, in the 11th regnal year, Payni of Pharaoh Ptolemaios (III.) LPH, son of Ptolemaios (II.) LPH. [_] Countersigned by Espmetis, son of Thotomous, to act in accordance with everything mentioned above, in the times mentioned above. [_] Countersigned by Harsiesis, son of Pouoptis: I will act in accordance with everything written above, in the times mentioned above. Harpaesis, son of Pouoptis, Espmetis, son of Thotomous, (and) Parates, son of Payris – yields 3 people – are the one who say unanimously: ⸢We⸣ [///]

translation_text: [en] [///] came [till?] now. I [///] have made the remaining costs(?). Not yet has any man [///]. It would be nice if you cause 6 (silver) kite to be brought to me, until one will serve(?) me within my house. Amun, the great god, will not allow that the priest of Osiris will be worried(?). If people are coming to me here who will be brought before the priest of Osiris, or if I (myself) travel to the south, I will bring them to your place, without anything having perished at the hand of the priest of Osiris. One knows the excellent instruction. For the translation cf. Den Brinker / Muhs / Vleeming, DBL (2005), 54. Written in the 13th regnal year, 7th of Epeiph.

translation_text: [en] He should take an oath before Satis, the Great, mistress of Elephantine: ‘Those are the 20½ (artabas) emmer which were given into the hand of Samaus. He has not given to me emmer therefrom except those to which he will agree.’ And Samaus shall take a vow(?) to him for the amounts of emmer. He shall take an oath [[///]]: ‘The birds which are in the stable(?) of Chnoum among the geese and which are (split) between me and Samaus: I have not taken away any bird from them. I have not caused (a bird) to be taken away except that to which he will agree.’ He shall take an oath: ‘The amounts of emmer which were given for the geese feeding of the geese of Samaus into my hand: I have not taken away emmer therefrom. I have not caused to be taken away emmer from the emmer, which was being intended(?) for the food of the geese and the fowl of the divine offering, except of that emmer to which we will agree. And he shall take the oath at the 30th of Mecheir.’ And Samaus shall take an oath for him [[///]] of Chnoum-the-breadwinner: ‘If I leave Elephantine without you having a partnership agreement with me (...) to give a thing to you which occurred for me outside. There is no fraud and falsehood within the oath.’

translation_text: [en] from the 18th regnal year, [///]th Mecheir onwards to note the freight costs of the measured emmer. Concerning the reckoning of the measured amounts of emmer one knows that one will be bound to give it (scil. the emmer) for the [geese] feedings and the duck feedings. Thus may remain for you the rest(?) to pay their rates. [It] was sent to Potemetous, the steward of Pharaoh, not to give you any emmer exept 5 (artabas) emmer daily. The amounts of emmer which were given (to) the wab priests of Isis from Philae and (to) the wab priests of Haroeris, together with the remaining emmer which was awarded: It should be caused to issue a receipt about it at the time of this morning(?). Translating according to Zauzich. We will take ourselves immediately to Elephantine. Written by Nechtminis, son of Sochotes, in the 18th regnal year, 18th of Mecheir.

translation_text: [en] To Eschnoum(p)metis, the lesonis. [_] It was sent to me on behalf of Cf. Zauzich, P. Berl. Eleph. 2 (1993), note to l. 2 of P. 13536: ı͗.ı͗r-ḥr cannot be translated here as „t“, but must introdice the sender or principal of the letter in question. the chief of Thebes the following: ‘Let them gather the emmer of the district Cf. Martin, Demotic Texts (1996), Nr. C8, for „district“ contra „nome“.of the Southern Land into the granaries, according to the receipt of measuring which was brought in the 5th regnal year, until they know what they have to deliver additionally . Reading variant Zauzich, P. Berl. Eleph. 1 (1978): „bringen soll.“ And they should collect (the emmer) until the 21st day of the month .’ Let them gather the emmer of the temple-domain NOTE according to that which is written above. The (same amount of) emmer which has delivered the temple-domain of Chnoum for Pharaoh in the 5th regnal year: Let it be brought into the granary of Pharaoh until the chief of Thebes arrives. And let this be ordered (officially)! Do not delay for one (single) hour while emmer is lying on the street! Written by Esminis in the 6th regnal year, 19th of Mecheir. To [_] Eschnom(p)metis.

May he write about everything what will happen with (the) temple of Elephantine [///]! to them(?). These loaves of bread which belong to him (and) regarding which he asked a question: The wab priest disposal amounts to 5 breads. The breads therefrom at the trouble Cf. Thesaurus Linguae Aegyptiae and Den Brinker / Muhs / Vleeming, DBL (2005), 54. [///] yields 8 ⅓ breads daily. He has spoken to me: ‘The general has ordered Es-Onuris-neb-scha to [///].’ in order to give it to him. The breads in question - Es-Onuris-neb-scha has ordered it therefore on declaration of [///] of the general. Pachnoumis has spoken to me, too: ‘I have sent to Eschnompmetis: Ask [///]!’ That on which we will say: ‘Do it in order to let the mentioned [wab priests] being safe!’: Do it! It is the case that Pachnoumis has said it to me [///] Voice of the servant Snebonychos, son of Espneti-hetnetjer, [before the carrier-of-Ra Espmetis, son of Espneti-hetnetjer].

translation_text: [en] [Voice of Espmet]sepsis [_] before his master, the carrier-of-Ra Chnoummachis, the scribe of the divine roll. I greet you before Ptah, the great god. Oh may he cause me to see you again without harm! [_] O may Pre cause his lifetime to be long! In the 16th regnal year [///] The matters which happened on this day, when the wab priests of Chnoum travelled to Memphis to visit the overseer of the field, will [not] be hidden from you from that day onwards when they happened. I have arrived before the overseer of the field. I have told him the matters happened [to me?]. He has spoken to me: ‘If you desire travel to the South, you are welcome! If you desire to stay here in Memphis, stay again!’ I have found out that my matters Lit."the things with me". could be strengthened if I would stay here in Memphis. Therefore, I stayed (in Memphis). Do not let anybody Cf. Den Brinker / Muhs / Vleeming, DBL (2005), 54. in the world make your heart sad by reason of a matter which happened here in Memphis unlawfully! Akky, son of Snebonychos, and Nepherpres, son of Harnouphis, will report everything which I have said to them before the scribe of the divine roll. They (scil. the words resp. matters) are too numerous to be written in a letter. Spotous, the lesonis, and the wab priests who accompany him tell me all circumstances which are well. I know the fact that he is entitled(?) to permit writing in my presence Cf. Den Brinker / Muhs / Vleeming, DBL (2005), 54. during the journey of the god of the gate. Nothing happens except the works of the god. Be unconcerned! I carry out work on the papyrus rolls wich are in my hand. I have met the scribes of the treasury, with whom meeting here is profitable They have told me what is suitable. I wrote this letter on the 23rd of Mecheir. I will deliver these letters written in my presence Cf. Den Brinker / Muhs / Vleeming, DBL (2005), 54. to the officials on the 24th (of Mecheir), together with Spotous, the lesonis. The high priest had not a word with me concerning the journey in the way of a man who knows another in person since former times. Age will tell you the mentioned matters. The wab priests have reached an agreement to hand over to me 1 silver (deben) and 2 (silver) kite, because(?) they were left over. The money and the castor oil, which they have brought to Memphis: It is he who apportioned so as not to allow According to Den Brinker / Muhs / Vleeming, DBL (2005), 54. giving it to me, speaking: ‘I will tell it before the overseer of the field. It will be given to him.’ As soon as the overseer of the field arrives, you shall appear before him. You shall tell him everything that has to be said and that is appropriate. It may happen that I go there for a talk(?). May it not happen that he takes offence at you! This is not a matter which you had ordered to me! But as soon as I arrive I will let you know that I have not triggered a scandal. In this letter, I have revealed nothing (in detail), so that no one is able to read it before it reaches you. You will hear them all on declaration of Age and Nepherpres. Do not worry about a matter which has nothing! By Chnoum, the Great, master of Elephantine! Anything appropriate escaped(?) the scribe of the divine roll, in order to cause it do be done to him for my sake during the journey by ship. I have received a fingertip(?) of ..?.., yields by estimate 2 silver (deben), 5 kite, (and) a fingertip of ..?.. I have delivered them into the hand of Nepherpres, son of Harnouphis, in order to bring them to you as soon as the god saves me. And I myself will bring (to you) everything which is worth bringing. I will send to you concerning the matters which will happen with us here. The(?) [///], May it be ordered to send to us concerning this! I have written this letter when Spotous, the lesonis, was on the move to Philae, together with the Chnoum priests because of the priests who ought to be elected as wab priests of Chnoum and [Satis(?)] Cf. Den Brinker / Muhs / Vleeming, DBL (2005), 54.. [Written in the xth regnal year], 24th of Mecheir. [Voice of Espmet]sepsis before his master, the carrier-of-Ra Chnoummachis, the scribe of the divine roll.

title: Pap. Berlin P. 13536

translation_text: [en] Chonompres greets the wab priests of Chnoum from Elephantine, the lesonis (and) the temple scribes. Oh may Neith cause their lifetime to be long! I had sent to you earlier that I was sent to by the chief lector: Thus the wab priests of Chnoum, the lesonis, (and) the temple scribes should be brought into that house which I am residing, on a day within ten days, beginning from the 24th regnal year, 16th of Mecheir. You did not come (to) Edfu into the house in which the chief lector is residing until today! [_] Once this letter reaches you, come to the house in which I am residing, with the revision of the temple in written form in your hands, three rolls together with the account of the offering donation of Chnoum for the 22nd, the 23rd and the 24th regnal year! [_] And go to the house in which the chief lector is residing! Do not let the appointed day that was sent to me by the chief lector elapse by the chief lector! [_] Written by Petobastis in the 24th regnal year, 6th of Phamenoth. (Chonompres) greets the wab priests of Chnoum of Elephantine, the lesonis (and) the temple scribes of Elephantine.

translation_text: [en] [Voice of Chnoummachis], son of Espmetsepsis, [before the carrier-of-Ra] Eschnompmetis, the priest of Hapi. Oh may Pre cause his lifetime to be long! I have reached the nome of Esna. I have visited the chief of the [Southern Land] concerning the offering income before Chnoum. I have spoken in front of him concerning emmer as emmer. He has said to me: ‘I will cause the chief of Thebes to come to the north. When I come back, I will cause to be given to you emmer as emmer. Let your agent stay here! I will not allow that something goes wrong with the offering income before Chnoum.’ I have hesitated, saying Cf. also Den Brinker / Muhs / Vleeming, DBL (2005), 53: “so that I might find”. (to me?) : Until I have determined his activities, take care of all matters being with you! Let the Pastophor arrive, who shall take [the] wreath of the chief of Thebes `at the 1st of Pachons´ and (the wreath) of his staff, and the wreath of the priest of Chnoum and (the wreath) of his staff and (the wreath) of the Great! They may deliver the dispatches of the nome scribes! Send to me (concerning) the matters happened to you! Issued by Chnoummachis in the 6th regnal year, 25th of Pharmuti. [To] Eschnompmetis, the priest of Hapi.

translation_text: [en] 5th regnal year, Paophi, of Pharaoh Psammetich LPH. Thus has spoken the stonemason and servant of Pharaoh Peteminis, son of Peteharpochrates and his mother Esharpochrates, to the blacksmith Harmais, son of Peteasychis and his mother Tahi: You have satisfied my heart with the amount of money for (my) red pregnant plowing cow which is branded with the brand of Isis and which is called by the cow name Ta-pa-uten, the daughter of (my) private cow, which was born in (my) corral. I have sold her to you for an amount of money. You have given me the money for her I have received it from your hand. My heart is satisfied with it. She belongs to you (now). She is your cow from today onward together with her calf which she shall bear in your corral. No man in the world, from father, mother, brother, sister, son, daughter, even myself, shall be enabled to wield power over her except you. As for the one who shall come to you in regard to her in order to take her from you saying: ‘She does not belong to you!’ (in) (my) name or (in) the name of any other man in the world, I will cause him to withdraw from you in regard to her. If I fail to cause him to withdraw from you in regard to her, I shall give you a similar red and pregnant plowing cow in exchange for her on the day when one might take her from you, while I [///] Esminis, son of Osoroeris. Horos, son of Iret-redj. Peteesis, son of Horos. Oaphres, son of Chapochrates.

translation_text: [en] [Voice of the servant Psintaes, son] of Es-Onuris-neb-scha, ⸢before⸣ [the scribes ..?.. . Oh may Pre cause] their lifetime [to be long!] I have made [///] [///] ⸢Espneti-hetnetjer. They have brought it⸣ [///] in the name of those dates. May it be ordered to allot it to Snebonychos in that (moment?) when he comes to you. And may it be ordered according to the income of my priesthood of Anoukis, to cause to give them into his hand at these dates! It was sent to you concerning it also in a letter of Cf. Zauzich, P. Berl. Eleph. 2 (1993), note to l. 2 of P. 13536: ı͗.ı͗r-ḥr cannot be translated here as „to“, but must introduce the sender or principal of the letter in question. the overseer of the field again. It was sent concerning it to the lesonis and the wab priests. It was sent to you by the overseer of the field concerning the payments of the mentioned endowments, which are (written) in the other roll: ‘They may be brought hither!’ May it be ordered them to be brought according to that about which it was sent to you Cf. Den Brinker / Muhs / Vleeming, DBL (2005), 54. by the overseer of the field! It was sent concerning it by the overseer of the field (to) Espmetis, the general, and (to) the wab priests (and to) the lesonis. It was ordered by the overseer of the field to Eschnompmetis, son of Psammetichos, the scribe of Chnoum, who arrived here together with the wab priests concerning the emmer, the wine and the (other) things which shall be given before Anoukis (in) the temple of Elephantine, to cause them(?) to be written ‘into my hand’ in the temple rolls. They were written into the rolls of the 2nd regnal year, which were brought to the overseer of the field in the 3rd regnal year. [They] are written in the nbnb(?) Otherwise unknown word, presumably a terminus technicus: Cf. Zauzich, P. Berl. Eleph. 2 (1993), P 13634 for line x+8: „ nbnb Ordnung?“. of the 3rd regnal year also. It was written in the instructions given by the overseer of the field in order to bring them to the temple of Elephantine: ‘They may act according to them (scil. the instructions mentioned above)!’ It was ordered by the overseer of the field also: ‘May it be searched for the other roll of Chnoum!’ The amounts of emmer, the wine, the meat, the birds (and) the further things which will be found, will be given (as offering) before Anoukis for the 2 daughters of Anoukis, in order to give them into the hand of (my) attorney. It was written in the orders which were brought to you also. That about which I send happened before the wab priests, who came to Memphis to the overseer of the field. May it be ordered to cause it to be acted according to them (scil. the orders)! And may it be ordered to send to me what will be done! And it may also be ordered to Eschnompmetis, son of Psammetichos, because of the payments concerning of which it was sent to you by Cf. Zauzich, P. Berl. Eleph. 2 (1993), note to l. 2 of P. 13536: ı͗.ı͗r-ḥr cannot be translated here as „to“, but must introduce the sender or principal of the letter in question. the overseer of the field: ‘Let them be brought here!’ Written by Psintaes, son of Es-Onuris-neb-scha, in the 3rd regnal year, 5th of Tybi. Voice [of the servant Psintaes, son of Es-Onuris-neb-scha,] before the scribes [///]

translation_text: [en] 8th regnal year, Thoth, of Pharaoh Ptolemaios (V.), son of Ptolemaios (IV.) and Arsinoë (III.), the gods Philopatores, when Konon, son of Ptolemaios, was wab priest of Alexander and the gods Soteres, the gods Adelphoi, the gods Euergetai, the gods Philopatores and the Pharaoh Ptolemaios (V.) Epiphanes, when Nesias, daughter of Apelles, was athlophore of Berenike Euergetis, [when T]hemisto, daughter of Hegesistratos, was kanephore before Arsinoë Philadelpha, when the lady Eirene, daughter of Ptolemaios, was wab priestess of Arsinoë Philopator, when Kleitomachos, son of Dikaiarchos, was wab priest in the nome of Thebes of Ptolemaios (I.) Soter and Pharaoh Ptolemaios (V.) Epiphanes. Thus has spoken the man of Aphonte, Petosiris, son of Patseous and his mother Tachnoumis, to the lady Senesis, daughter [of Petepnebtous] and her mother Telaias: I have made you a wife. I have given to you 3 deben, yields 15 stater, yields 3 deben again, as your dowry. The children, whom you will bear to [me], and Peteharoeris, (my) eldest son, and the lady Tayoui NOTE, his sister, (my) children whom you bore to me, are the owners of all things and everybody which belong to me, together with that which I will acquire (henceforth), together with the goods of father and mother in field, temple (and) the city. If I leave you as wife and if I prefer [another] woman to you, I shall give you 5 deben, yields 25 staters, yields 5 deben again, additionally to 3 deben mentioned above, which I have given to you as your dowry, in total 8 deben, yields 40 staters, yields 8 deben again. This is the inventory of the woman’s possessions, which you brought with you into (my) house: 1 silver(?) necklace: Yields 3 deben, in the name of your dowry mentioned above. 2 pairs of golden ..?..: yields an amount of gold by estimate: 5¼ kite. 1 pair of gswr-rings, yields an amount of gold by estimate: 2¼ kite. 1 pair of glṱ-pnꜥ-rings, yields an amount of gold [by estimate:] 2 [kite]. 1 ..?..: Yields an amount of gold by estimate: ½ kite. Yields (total) gold by estimate: 1 deben, yields 5 stater, yields gold by estimate as 1 deben again. 1 wool garment: Yields 30 deben. 1 dress: Yields 20 deben. 1 shawl(?): yields 5 (deben). 1 generty-shety garment: Yields 10 deben. 1 her-sehre: Yields 2 deben and 8 kite. 1 ..?..: yields 10 deben. 1 ladle(?):1 deben. Another (ladle): 1 deben. 1 medjeg jug: deben. 1 ladle(?): 1 deben. 1 aysabelle (piece of jewelry?), which is inscribed (painted?) ..?..: Yields an amount of gold by estimate: 1½ kite. Money in cash NOTE: 1 (deben), yields 5 staters, yields 1 deben again. Worked [copper]: 1 large vessel: Yields by estimate 10 deben. 1 small vessel: Yields by estimate 2 deben. 1 mirror: Yields by estimate 10 deben. 1 brazier: Yields by estimate 30 deben. 1 bs vessel: Yields by estimate 1 deben and 5 kite. Total value of the above mentioned things: Gold by estimate: Yields 1 deben and 1½ kite, yields 5½¼ staters, yields gold by estimate: 1 (deben) and 1½ kite again. Money in cash: 1 (deben); copper: 86 (deben) and 8 kite. Total of money in cash and copper: 87 (deben) and 8 kite, yields 439 staters, yields 87 deben and 8 kite again. Worked copper: by estimate 53 (deben) and 5 kite. [This is the total of the] dowry which you brought with you into my house. I have received them from your hand, they are complete without any remainder. My heart is satisfied with them. If you will leave, I shall give them to you. If I will leave you as wife, I shall also give them to you, with necessity and without delay. Yours are the third of everything, and each thing which shall be between you and I from today henceforth Any man in the world who shall throw you out of my house and remove my possessions from your presence, he will fulfill to you the right (scil. the regulations) of this document-of-wife mentioned above, which I issued to you, with necessity and without delay or objection. Add to your dowry: 1 bowl, yields copper by estimate: 5 (deben). Countersigned by Petosiris, son of Patseous, himself NOTE : ‘I will act in accordance with everything written above.’ Issued by Espmetis, son of Esyris, who is scribe in Elephantine and Syene. For Tennesis. [list of 16 witnesses, very heavily faded]

translation_text: [en] because it happened that you are the thief ..?.. and you [///] He accused(?) them of the bad matter which is coming. Your enemies are not right. If I cease to be attorney of Eschnom(p)metis, the wab priests will not be able to give me bread for eating. And furthermore: If I had not been the attorney of Eschnom(p)metis, while the one who should give me bread cheated Eschnom(p)metis, I would (still) be the attorney for him. And furthermore: There is no one among you who would give offense to me in order to injure him(?). The lesonis has not withdrawn from you, when dispute rose (again?). As soon as it is set in your heart that he gives offense: This letter which he has written to you – he made it when I lived with him. I know that he made it (the letter). But he made it before the rate of the (amounts of) emmer were fixed. I have requested him to write a letter for you. He has ordered Hartophnachthes: ‘Write them the following: All that which I or Osoroeris say, may it happen!’ If it is said that he has not sent what will be ordered, I will be bound to send to you concerning this. But as I have sent ‘It is not ..?.. what I have against you’, I cannot ask them here. Otherwise I would have said it in Bige! I have sent to you again as follows: You are not used to make some loaves of bread as income.’ Until tomorrow appears and you send for emmer and the scribes will not let it to be brought to you, I will {not} send again. You have sent that you have sent concerning the expense for 33 baked breads, although (only) 30 baked breads are that which Psammetichos gives, not 9 breads related to one quarter(?). Furthermore: Inaros, son of Eschnoumis, (also) does not give 33 baked breads for ½ (artabas) emmer here! And they say For the translation cf. Den Brinker / Muhs / Vleeming, DBL (2005), 54.: ‘They (scil. we) have reached Bige, and 30 baked breads are that what is given for ½ (artaba) emmer! He who gives ⅙ (artabas) wheat to the baker, to whom he (scil. the baker) will give 8 baked breads.’ So I would say: ‘You know the actual situation.’ Thus has spoken the priest of Chnoum: ‘He who has ordered it, is before me, while it is another man who sent to me concerning it.’, because you know that Nechtminis has ordered: ‘Thus should be given 66 baked breads for 1 (artaba) emmer!’ But Psammetichos is a rich/ gentle man. The breads which he will not give to you, (for them) he will deposit emmer again. (Something) different: No emmer will be brought to him until he has not delivered them (scil the breads) completely. You have sent to me that you have disposed me as attorney of Nechtminis. I might say: ‘I was attorney of the lesonis.’ Teos has not sent to me concerning this. If it would be suitable for me to let you succeed Nepherpres, the priest of Chnoum, I would let you succeed him. I would have said: ‘Send to me (concerning) these matters!’ You have sent to me: ‘May the attorney of the lesons come up! May he make a reply here!’ Any attorney belonging to him will be able to be here. I would say (to him) to come up to you. You have sent: ‘If the Medes do not kill us, you will find out the disgracefulness of these things of the wab priests!’ Be aware of owning those (things) of which they want to take possession. But If I had desired to do damage, then I would have not written to you a letter. There are 16 baked breads which are regularly given to you in Bige. And you should give 4 baked breads therefrom as fee of Haroeris. And the other 12 baked breads should be deposited for you as replacement for those concerning Which wit was sent to me. You have sent: ‘I have given 7 (pieces) of meat to Haryotes, son of Teos.’ That is what is correct with them(?). Because it has been recognized that it angers you, I will not send to you (again) concerning something until the lesons arrives. Issued by Osoroeris, son of Teos, (in) the 18th regnal year, 26th of Mecheir.

translation_text: [en] Arrived is Phomtisosiris, son of Pa-[///]. I have heard] the matters which happened to [you, and the matters] which happened to the servants of Chnoum, [and the] grain, which was taken from them. I have addressed the matters in question According to Martin, Demotic Texts (1996). Reading variant Thesaurus Linguae Aegyptiae: „nämlichen“.to the priest of Chnoum. He has sent to Nechtminis, the priest of Min, concerning the fields of the divine offering Reading variant Zauzich, P. Berl. Eleph. 1 (1978): „Tempelgut“.of Chnoum, in order not to allow any man on earth to touch them. He Scil. the priest of Chnoum. has sent to Psintaes, son of Estphenis, not to touch the divine offering of Chnoum at all. I have spoken to him on account of the 256 (artabas) of barley which were taken from them. He has spoken to me: ‘They (the 256 artabas) should be brought out of their houses. It is not their seed-grain.’ Do not let your heart be saddened concerning the matters which have come. Nechtminis, the priest of Min, will not let a matter of yours go wrong. It is said that the priest of Chnoum will travel downstream here in Thebes on 1st Mesore. When he starts, I will send to you regarding that which should happen there. Issued by Potemetous, son of Psintaes, in the 17th regnal year, 29th of Epeiph. To [_] [///]

translation_text: [en] Farandata, to whom Egypt is entrusted, speaks to all the wab priests of Khnum, master of Elephantine. Now, Farandata is the one who speaks: There are the wab priests whom the leader of the first phyle brought in front of (me) earlier, saying: ‚One may appoint them as lesonis priests!‘ Since one of the mentioned wab priests fled, [_] it was ordered to seek for him. There is (another) one amongst them, who was servant of another man. Such people cannot be appointed as lesonis! Lit.: "es geziemt sich nicht, sie zum Lesonis zu machen" (according to Thesaurus Linguae Aegyptiae). Now, the wab priest whom it is suitable to be appointed as lesonis, a great man, whom I will allow to officiate (as a lesonis), there being nothing which he has let fail (this is the one), who shall be choosen according to that what Dareios LPH Pharaoh has ordered. Such (a person) can be appointed as lesonis! Lit.: "die Art von diesem ist es, die sich ziemt, ihn zu machen zum Lesonis" (according to Thesaurus Linguae Aegyptiae). Now, the wab priest who shall be choosen to be made the lesonis is like this. That one who will be chosen, will be brought Reading according to Martin, Demotic Texts (1996), Nr. C1. according to that which Dareios LPH Pharaoh has ordered. The wab priest whom it shall happen that there is something which he has let fail, or the one who is servant of another man: Such (men) do not let them summoned (to me) to be appointed as lesonis! Take not of that! Lit.: "möge es geschehen, dass es euch bekannt ist!" (according to Thesaurus Linguae Aegyptiae). Satabara knows this instruction. Peftjauawineith is the one who has written this letter. Countersigned by Oaphres, in the 30th regnal year, 29th of Choiak. [Letter According to Martin, Demotic Texts (1996), no. C1. to] all wab priests of Khnum, [master of Ele]phantine, from Farandata, to whom Egypt [is entrusted].

translation_text: [en] [34th regnal year … of Pharaoh Amasis]. Thus has spoken the cataract pilot Hapertais, son of Teos and his mother Taiu-nacht, (to) the lady Seniah, daughter of Pa-anch-i-ir-iy and her mother Tetesatis: (I) have made you a wife in the 34th regnal year. [///] [/// The children whom you will bear to me] are sharers with my children who are (already) born, concerning all and each which belongs to me, together with that which I will gain, together with the estates of father (and) mother. If I leave (you) as [wife ///] [/// I shall pay out to you 2 silver (deben) from] the treasury of Thebes, together with 8 (sacks) emmer (by the measure) of 40 hin. Written by Espmetis, son of Psammetichos-menechib. Oaphres, son of Haryotes. Espotethotes, son of Esyris. Espmetis, son of Esonnophris. Ithoros, son of Haryotes. Psammetichos, son of ⸢Oaphres-meri-neithes⸣. Peteteti, son of Peteyris. [///].

translation_text: [en] Voice of Hartophnachthes, son of Snebonychos, before the carrier-of-Ra Eschnompmetis. Oh may Pre cause his lifetime to be long! Espmetis, the mḏre, has brought me three letters, (among them) a letter for Pa-ir-dit, son of Bereh. He has brought two (letters) for me. I have read them. I have heard all matters about which you have sent. You have sent concerning those who are elected as wab priests, in order to write in the temple Reading variant Zauzich, P. Berl. Eleph. 2 (1993): „… um (sie) im Tempel (ein)zuschreiben.“. That with which you are satisfied together with the wab priests holding office: I am satisfied with it (also). You have sent: “Look, they have billed it(?). Send to the wab priests according to the mentioned matters! Reading variant Zauzich, P. Berl. Eleph. 2 (1993): „man hat abgerechnet.‘ Schreib (auch) an die Priester die nämlichen Worte!“ Stop(?) considering it as your matter According to Zauzich, P. Berl. Eleph. 2 (1993). Thesaurus Linguae Aegyptiae reads here „?“. only Cf. Den Brinker / Muhs / Vleeming, DBL (2005), 52., because it is a matter of the four phyles!’ But Pa-hetnetjer, son of Eschnompmetis, is a wise man. He is a man able to conduct the investigation of a matter, without causing irritation(?) among the wab priests and the god himself. I have agreed with him. The god has agreed with him, too. But I do not know, who is selected in the heart of the wab priests. The men who are here will be wary of doing something which is not suitable. But if you send concerning something to this effect, Comment in Thesaurus Linguae Aegyptiae: (eigentl. "geschrieben habt" = "geschrieben haben werdet"). one may order not to allow to raise a plea (against) allowing him to investigate in the temple, in the town (and) in (every) place on earth. And one may order to give him the books whom he desires to carry out the investigation of the failed matters. Matters from which the god benefits are they. When you have sent to me According to Zauzich, P. Berl. Eleph. 2 (1993). concerning Osoroeris: ‘You have quarrelled with him’ by sending to {me} him : ‘You beware of nobody’, I would rather have said (to) Osoroeris: ‘Be aware of the god!’ This (would be the case) not to hurt him. But because I was aware of him, it was appropriate to be aware of me, too. Concerning that you have sent to me: ‘Nobody is doing something for which he can be seized.’ I am aware that there was something which you are used to saying and which I do not know. But the people who hate (me) are those who say them. I am not in Elephantine today, neither in my name nor in the name of Osoroeris According to Thesaurus Linguae Aegyptiae. Reading variant by Den Brinker / Muhs / Vleeming, DBL (2005), 52: „my name is not | the name of Osoroeris“., son of Teos. The people saying the slander(?) do not remain hidden (from me). Those who reveal things which are worth doing for me: They are with Osoroeris. I have taken my 2 (artabas) wheat and the 2 (artabas) barley into his house. They are at his expense. Provide me as a reward(?) a favour, because he writes in the temple. He attacks me and them with obloquy, and he brings a disturbance(?) Thesaurus Linguae Aegyptiae gives here only „?“, Zauzich, P. Berl. Eleph. 2 (1993) translates “Unwetter(?)”. to the house of Espneti-hetnetjer and of Chnemeus(?) . According to Thesaurus Linguae Aegyptiae. Reading variant Zauzich, P. Berl. Eleph. 2 (1993): „Chnoum-em-hapi“. The things which I have not said will disgrace my name. He(?) will repay(?) (it), the god, to cause to take away his assets, too. I did not agreewith him in written form(?), [_] the wab priests formerly (not), too.

translation_text: [en] To Hartophnachthes, the chief of the sacred wrappings. Oh may Pre cause his lifetime to be long! It was brought to me, this letter. I have heard about your well-being. I have heard all that about which you have sent. I have caused Payris, son of Adeli-ye, to be brought. I have encouraged him greatly on your account. The other matters concerning which you have sent: I know them. Take care! Perform the worship until the god causes a favorable outcome to happen. Thus has complained Chnoum-..?.., son of Pasenis against Pamounis and Artais (and) Esyris (and) the other people who desire trouble with us. I have encountered much conflict since I came to Syene to the general for the desire to cause the people of Aphonte to succeed me. Those who disputed with me, (dispute) with the god as well. I am told through all people who are (in) Elephantine (and) Syene: ‘There is no woman in your house!’ If it is pleasing to you, you may take a man with you and consult the oracle of Isis concerning the woman who I should take home: The woman Tayris, daughter of Pachnoumis, or the woman Tareshy, daughter of Espmetis. And send me the answer for reinforcement! This was a very important matter, (otherwise) I would not have sent something like this to you. If it happens, that here is a something (which is desired): One may send to me concerning it. Written in the 3rd regnal year, 7th of Hathyr. Intentional space, 9 lines high To [_] Hartophnachthes.

translation_text: [en] To Parates, son of Inaros. I greet you here before Osiris, Horus (and) Isis, the gods of Abydos [who will grant] that [I] stand before you without harm. This letter has been brought to me. I have heard about your well-being and about the matters concerning which you have sent to me. Snebonychos, the priest of Sachmet, will tell you about the nature NOTE of the services which I have done on his order. You have sent to me concerning the matters with the couple of doors. You have not sent concerning their price. If you have found a ship(?) NOTE which is on the way to the north, it may bring them! And you should send to me concerning their price so that I might not give it (over) to the hand of Taha. One may accomplish it well! Do not let it be delayed, because I need them very urgently NOTE! And you should also send to me (concerning) their transport price. There are two men from Per-uqer who were brought to the south among the men from Per-uqer, who NOTE belong to a small garden (and) who are used to deliver to me some wine out of it. We do not know the place to which they are brought, while NOTE it is said to them: ‘Guard the two men!’ May it be ordered to ask for the place to which they will be brought! And you should engage yourself in them, and you should take care that you can cause one to send [to us ] NOTE concerning them. If it happens that here is something which one will desire: let it be ordered to send to us concerning it. Written in the 3rd regnal year, 23rd of Epeiph. To Parates, [_] son of Inaros.

translation_text: [en] Voice of Osoroeris, son of Espmetis, before the carrier-of-Ra Eschnompmetis, son of Chnoummachis, scribe of the divine book. We have heard something about Nepher-pres. We do not trust Es-Haryotes. One may ask about the well-being of the temple of Elephantine and about the further items. And you may send to me concerning them. The matters about which I have spoken to you, I have handed them over to Pasenis in order to bring them to you. If it suits to cause the lesonis to take them for barley, they may be given to him! If it suits to cause the chief-of-field to take them, they may be given to him ! I have agreed with everything that you shall do. The rings which you will take for you: May each of you give a ring into the hand of Pasenis! If it happens that he has found a man Cf. Den Brinker / Muhs / Vleeming, DBL (2005), 53., to receive it (scil. the ring?), send to us! If he has not requested (him), then keep it with you! No man believes in Es-Haryotes. Do not rely on him! There is no barley (and no) emmer here for the offering supply. Es-Haryotes is not the one who gave the copper money. On another day it shall be counted with us altogether. One may ask about (my) well-being Reading variant Zauzich, P. Berl. Eleph. 2 (1993): „Möge man (das Orakel) befragen nach meinem Heil“. while I am staying here (in) Elephantine! Reading variant Zauzich, P. Berl. Eleph. 2 (1993): „ob ich hier auf Elephantine bleiben soll.“ If it is appropriate to come up, I will come again. Written at the 29th day.

translation_text: [en] Inner script 1| [23rd regnal year, 28th of Thoth of Pharaoh Ptolemaios (III.), son of Ptolemaios (II.), and of the Female Pharaoh Berenike (II.), the gods Euergetai.] 2| [The priest of Hor-]…, priest of Ptah, priest of the gods Euergetai, Teos-Eponychos Junior, son of Harpaesis 3| [and the likewise entitled Psintaes, son of Patous, altogether two persons], are the ones who speak [to] [Euphro]nios praktor: 4| ‘[Our payment] is guaranteed, a man relating to his companion, for giving according to P]inyris, son of [Estphenis] 963 [silver kite], which are the value 5| [in] copper of the [¼] of the grain, which should be paid [to] the temple of Edfou from the 16th regnal year, 1st of Hathyr, to 6| [the 17th regnal year], 30th of Paophi: Yields 12 ⅙ month, yields 1 year. The ¼ [of the] same grain mentioned above, [he was] admitted culpability 7| [according to it by the] scribes [of] Hor-Behedeti, the gods Adelphoi, the gods Euergetai, to Estphenis, 8| [son of Pete-Harsi-]ese. Outer script 9| [23rd regnal year], 28th of Thoth of Pharaoh Ptolemaios (III.), son of Ptolemaios (II.), 10| [and of the] Female Pharaoh Berenike (II.), the gods] Euergetai, 11| [The priest of Hor-]…, priest of Ptah, priest of the gods Euergetai, Teos-Eponychos 12| [the son] of Harpaesis and the likewise entitled Psintaes, son of Patous, altogether two persons, are the ones who speak to 13| [Euphro]nios praktor: ‘Our payment is guaranteed, 14| [a man relating to his] companion, [for giving according to P]inyris, son of Estphenis, for] 963 [silver kite], which are the value 15| [in copper] of the ¼ of the grain, which should be paid to the temple of Edfou from the 16th regnal year, 16| 1st [of Hathyr, to the 17th regnal year], 30th of Paophi: Yields 12 ⅙ month, yields 1 year. The ¼ of 17| the same [grain] mentioned above, he was admitted culpability according to it by the scribes of 18| [Hor-Beh]edeti, the gods Adelphoi, the gods Euergetai, [in front of] Estphenis, 19| [son of Pete]-Harsi-]ese, who inquires regarding the sanctuaries of the gods in the Southern Land. We will pay it (scil. the grain) in full 20| [to the temple of] Edfou from 23rd regnal year, 30th of Hathyr, to the completion 21| [of] 4 [years]: Yields 48 ⅔ month, after we were given in its exchange his patrimony, his houses, his 22| [vacant] lots, his income, his payment(?), his temple shares, his 23| share [certifications, his] income certifications. If we are given the items mentioned above, 24| [we will] pay in full his amounts of money mentioned above on their payment date of the four years mentioned above. 25| [The amount of money] from them, which we will not give like the fee of this four years mentioned above: 26| [We will give it] (to the extent of) 100 kite (copper) respectively on 2 [kite (silver), [yields] 48[1500 in that] month, which is after 27| the same [month], in this four years mentioned above, [with necessity] and without delay. We shall not 28| [be able] to give you another [payment] date concerning them [after] the four years mentioned above. 29| [Everything and every item], which belongs to us, and that, which we shall purchase, shall hold in pledge for all words 30| [mentioned above], until we do for you their justice at [any] time. If you stand behind the one whom you prefer 31| [of us], the 2 persons, he may act for you in accordance with all words mentioned above. (But) if you prefer to be 32| [behind] us, the 2 persons, you will be there, too. And we will act for you such, those who you preferred, 33| [a man] as well as the 2 persons. It is you or your representative, who is believed regarding all items, 34| [regarding] all [items], about which he will speak with us on behalf of [all] items mentioned above. And we do them on his declaration 35| [with necessity] and without delay, without any exercise of influence. 36| [Countersigned by the priest of Hor-…, priest of] Ptah, priest of the gods Euergetai, [Teos-Epo]nychos, [son] of Harpaesis, 37| [accordingly: ‘I will act corresponding to [all words] mentioned above.’ Countersigned by the [likewise entitled Psintaes, son of] Patous, [his] companion, 38| [accordingly: ‘I will] act corresponding to all words mentioned above.’ 39| 23rd regnal year, 28th of Thoth. In Edfou [///] 40| for Pinyris, son of Estphenis, high priest of the 16th year: The sureties, that they give 41| the fourth part of grain in the amount of(?) 3 silver talents and 1260 (drachmas), 42| … silver drachmas, not annually. Tharephonychos junior, son 44| of Harpaesis [///] Vso 1 [///] Psenaies, son of Patus Vso 2| priest [///] Vso 3| tax collection for the royal land … Vso 4| … of Tharephonychos [///] Vso 5| … Vso 6| … Vso 7| Pryr, son of Estphenis, high priest of the 16th year [///] Vso 8| sureties [///]

translation_text: [en] 3rd regnal year, Thoth, of Pharaoh Ptolemaios - son of Ptolemaios, son of P[tolemaios - and of the Female Pharaoh For this title cf. also Moje, Herrscherfrauen (2013), with further literature. Berenike, the hauler of light. Thus has spoken the lady Tahapis, daughter of Kellos and her mother ..?.., to the lady Tastis, daughter of Haronnophris and her mother Esoeris: I have taken a lawsuit against you before the judges on account of this house, which is situated in the central quarter of Elephantine, and (on account of) these household effects, and (on account of) this necklace from silver. The judges have ruled against me concerning them For the translation cf. Den Brinker / Muhs / Vleeming, DBL (2005), 203.. I have withdrawn from you concerning the house mentioned above, which was given against repayment to the lady Tayga, and (concerning to) these household effects (and) the necklace. I have nothing in the world against you in their name as of today, in every year forever. The one who will come [against you Sethe, Bürgschaftsrecht (1920), Nr. „zu dir“.] concerning them in my name, in the name of any man on earth (or) in the name of the consent which you made Cf. Den Brinker / Muhs / Vleeming, DBL (2005), 204 after Sethe: „im Namen des Rufens, das du getan hast“. regarding the document which was issued to Tayga (concerning) the mentioned house Sethe, Bürgschaftsrecht (1920): „in seinem (Scil. des Hauses) Namen“., I will cause him to withdraw from you, the necklace and the household effects ..?.. The version of Sethe „die sie gegeben hat für ihren [Anteil] am Begräbnis der Böcke“ is doubted in Den Brinker / Muhs / Vleeming, DBL (2005), 204, as well as the reading of Spiegelberg „[welches] sie als meinen Begräbnisanteil…“. Martin, Demotic Texts (1996), no. C 31 gives no translation.. You have executed their law, their judgment, their oath for me before Chnoum. I have nothing in the world against you in their name Scil. „of the house and the property“.. Your attorney is the one who is to be entrusted concerning everything about which he shall speak with me in the name of everything mentioned above. I am doing them at his behest with necessity and without delay, without any malice. The lady Tayga, daughter of [Pa]tus, to whom the above mentioned house was given, shall be behind Cf. Den Brinker / Muhs / Vleeming, DBL (2005), 204.Tastis, daughter of Haronnophris, on account of the document-of-sale and the document-of-withdrawal, of which Tastis said: ‘I will cause Tahapis, daughter of Kellos Cf. Den Brinker / Muhs / Vleeming, DBL (2005), 203., to issue Cf. Den Brinker / Muhs / Vleeming, DBL (2005), 204. them, and I will issue them to the lady Tayga, daughter of Patus.’ According to Martin, Demotic Texts (1996), no. C 31. Written by the pastophor of Amun, Imouthes, son of Amenothes. 3rd regnal year, Thoth. Document-of-withdrawal which Kapis issued to Tastis on account of the lawsuit. Lilos, son of Hor; Paous, son of Harsiesis; Psenchonsis, son of Espmetis; Horos, son of Horos, son of Haryotes; Psammetichos-sa-Neith, son of the choachyt Saneith; Pachnoumis, son of Petearensnouphis; Imouthes, son of Belles; Patous, son of Hor; Petearensnouphis, son of Pelaias; Paesis, son of Pasenis; Psenpoeris, son of Peteyris; Phomtis, son of Horaam; Anch-mer-wer, [son] of Psenhapis; Espmetis, son of Pelaias; Petearensnouphis, son of Pouoptis; Thotsytmis, son of Petosiris.

translation_text: [en] [Eschnompmetis,] son of Snebonychos, is the one who speaks: We have caused Parates, son of Inaros, to come, he who belongs to the wab priests of Chnoum, the gods Adelphoi, the gods Euergetai (and) the gods Philopatores, in order to travel to Alexandria, with the garlands which shall be presented before Pharaoh at the victory festival of Pharaoh on the 10th of Pachons. If it is pleasing that the priest of Horus personally Cf. Martin, Demotic Texts (1996) C13. sends to let him move out from the fortress (scil. Alexandria ), According to Martin, Demotic Texts (1996), no.C13. together with his pastophor without restraining that which is instructed in this regard, let it be ordered to the authorized representative to send to me concerning it. Written in the 6th regnal year, 29th of Payni. [///]⸢..?..⸣ the letter [_] ⸢..?..⸣

translation_text: [en] To Teos, son of Pachnoumis. [_] Thus Maseratef, the priest of Sokar, has sent to Teos, the priest of Chons: ‘There are some people, who are omitted from the census lists of the district of Elephantine in the 5th regnal year.’ He has spoken with the high priest. He is uttermost persistent in this case, while everybody (else) is on the festival of Mut at the 8th of Payni [_]. He has commanded Peteminis, son of Sisiois: ‘Teos should add them to the list before you are even with his scribe!’ I have spoken to him: ‘My scribe has not yet come to the south!’ He has sent to Maseratef not to be even with Hakes and Psenyris, your scribe, before they have added the said persons to the list. I send you their list below. He has sent to the [///] again to cause you to give their money doubled, [///] will cause you to take note of the following: ‘Everything displeasing(?) used to be found in the field of [Elephantine]!’ [_] Furthermore, concerning the affairs of the measurement receipt from which you have subtracted 230 (artabas) of wheat, brought by Patous, son of Poeris, who came before Mnesarchos (and) Teos, the priests of Khonsu, when they came hither, and when Demetrios was caused to take aroyal oath concerning them, and when I was caused to sign them, and when I objected to them: They are too numerous to send about them. [_] Mnesarchos has spoken: ‘It is the scribe who is entitled to commit an oath to him.’ I have not found anything on this. I was caused to sign the royal oath concerning the grain, in which the 230 (artabas) wheat are included. Thus has said to me Patous, son of Poeris: ‘There was an agreement with me and Teos, son of Pachnoumis. If I do not incriminate them therein, I will be liable(?).’ [_] It may happen that you cause to bring to me a papyrus containing the measurement receipt including the 230 (artabas) of wheat, and that you cause a receipt to be issued (for it)! If there is something on it which the people had given to Patous, son of Poeris, he should be caused to give it (back). [_] But if you object to them, send to me immediately in order that it happens that Demeterios, the tax collector, can bring it to Syene with him. I will cause him to speak with you about it, because the mentioned grain is part of the receipt which one caused the strategos to give him until the 30th of Payni. Written by Teos in the 5th regnal year, 9th of Payni. [_] The census lists which are reduced in the money disposal from the 5th regnal year: The salt tax: 149 men: Yields 4 deben, 9⅔ kite. 200 and 4 women: [_] Yields 2 deben, 5½ kite. [_] Yields 7 deben, 5⅙ kite. The liturgy tax: [_] 74 men: [_] Yields 7 deben, {3} SPITZKLAMMER 4 SPITZKLAMMER kite. The cloth tax: [_] 77 women: Yields 1 deben, 7 ⅔ 1/22 kite, ½ ¼ obols. Yields (total): 16 deben, 5 5/6 1/22 kite, ½ ¼ obols.

translation_text: [en] (To) Snebonychos, the high priest. Thus the wab priests have spoken to me as follows: ‘We have 2 days of construction work here.’ If Psenpelaias, the master builder is able to come up and execute the two days construction work, he may come! And it should happen that he will come down at the day of the water benediction in order to clean himselfin the temple. You have sent on account of this to Tegemi (and/ son of?) Pachnoumis. Sad is the appearance(?) of an insolence(?) According to Thesaurus Linguae Aegyptiae. Reading variant Zauzich, P. Berl. Eleph. 2 (1993): „Frechheit(?)“. which hurts the god. You should bring a man, who presents his matter without hurting the god Lit.: „... der seine Sache sagt, indem darin nichts ist, was gegen den Gott schlägt (o.ä.)).“ (according to Thesaurus Linguae Aegyptiae).. I am not informed about what has happened concerning this ½ silver kite which was possessed by Petemous and Imouthes. Issued by Eschnompmetis in the 1st regnal year, 9th of Pharmuti. ´To Snebonychos, [_] the high priest.
