translation_text: [en] Voice of the servants, the wab priests of Chnoum the Great, master of Elephantine, before Farandata, to whom Egypt is entrusted: We perform the blessings of Farandatabefore Khnum, the great god. Oh, may Chnoum cause his lifetime to be long! It happened on the 29th regnal year (of Dareios I.), Pharmouthi, the time for appointing a successor to the lesonis, that we had disposed Petechnoumis, son of Oaphres, who was lesonis. We had caused Eschnompmetis, son of Harchebis, to follow him as lesonis. We are in agreement [to appoint him as] lesonis. He will cause supplies of offerings and burnt-offerings to be presented before Chnoum. Issued by Espmetis, son of Esyris, the chief of the (sacred) wrappings, in the 30th regnal year, 2nd of Thoth. Voice of the servants, the wab priests of Chnoum the great, master of Elephantine, before Farandata, to whom Egypt is entrusted.

translation_text: [en] Voice of Horos, son of Pa-hetnetjer before his master, the high priest: I perform your blessings before Amun, the great god. Oh may Pre cause his lifetime to be long! Thus has arrived Psenpoeris here. I have heard about your well-being and the worries which you are doing on my account. It is nice that we are with you as a younger brother For the translation cf. Den Brinker / Muhs / Vleeming, DBL (2005), 49.. Concerning their remaining matters, do not let contempt arise between you and Pa-hetnetjer anymore! He used to say words with his mouth, which are not in his heart. It is nice that you come to the god, by your heart being satisfied.For the translation cf. Den Brinker / Muhs / Vleeming, DBL (2005), 49. There are other people who are saying certain matters. Do not let them scoff at you Lit.: „Do not let them rejoicing over you!“! As for everything which on earth happens: Every man is the brother of his neighbor Lit.: "There is no man except a man with his brother".. If you consider this, you will find it. The wab priests which are here, they have inquired about your well-being. They Scil. „the words“, but cf. the rejected variant „scil. the priests“ in Den Brinker / Muhs / Vleeming, DBL (2005), 49. are too much to send to you about it. I never found that they have not taken your order after you have left Lit.„after you set sail.“. If it happens that here According to Den Brinker / Muhs / Vleeming, DBL (2005), 49. Zauzich, P. Berl. Eleph. 1 (1978): „dort“. is something (which is desired): May it be ordered to send to me about it. Written in the 1st regnal year, 6th of Mesore. To [_] the high priest

translation_text: [en] [the matters], about which they [have sent] to me. He has caused [me to hear about the troubles] which the wab priests were in, [and the] harm which had happened to him earlier. More sad (than those troubles) is the detention in which I am still, and from which I am unable to get out. By [///] Reading variant ⸢ꜥnḫ⸣ -?- „so wahr … lebt“. Cf. Martin, Demotic Texts (1996), Nr. C8, he supposed for the word in the gap Ptḥ or Skr., if I could go into the district Cf. Martin, Demotic Texts (1996), Nr. C8, for „district“ contra „nome“., I would have done everything which I could do for you for your sustenance. I have asked about the journeys of Sirthotes, son of Harnouphis. I have been told: ‚He has not yet returned to Philae.‘ (Otherwise) I would have come to Philae Reading variant Martin, Demotic Texts (1996) C8: wn-nꜣ.w-ı͗w=y ı͗y „I was coming to Philae“., to inflict all harm to him which I could do to him by reason of the affairs in question. I have reported the affairs in question to Spotous, the lesonis. That which will be able to be done here, I shall cause it to be done. Do not let the heart Collective, in effect the plural should be used here. of the wab priests to be saddened, after this day has appeared. They are saying several words which are pleasing. Written by Potemetous, son of Psintaes in the 19th regnal year, 22nd of Thot.

translation_text: [en] [NN, son of] Peteesis is the one, who recites the blessings [(for) Petosor]smetis, (son) of Bienchis, here before Chnoum-Nikephoros. He may rescue you, he may `cause´ you to be saved, and he may cause every right thing to happen to you, and he may cause us to see Reading variant Thesaurus Linguae Aegyptiae: „dein Gesicht“.you without harm in good fortune {harmless in good fortune}. There is no harm with us here. We carry out the work (in) your name. Moreover, the matters of him on which you have spoken to us concerning the priest of Chnoum: We shall carry out all of them. The matter which you will desire here Cf. Den Brinker / Muhs / Vleeming, DBL (2005), 50.: Send to us concerning it! For translation after F. Hoffmann cf. Den Brinker / Muhs / Vleeming, DBL (2005), 50. Written in the 40th regnal year of Caesar (Augustus), 15th of Phamenoth. Let one ask about the well-being of Lilos, Greek name Λιλως. (of) Petechnoumis Reading variant Zauzich, P. Berl. Eleph. 1 (1978): „des Sohnes des Petechnoumis“, and (of) Pachnoumis Junior! No disadvantage is there with you daughter It was taken to Elephantine at the 27th day, in order to give it to Petosorsmetis, (son) of Bienchis, the priest.

translation_text: [en] A memorandum by Pinyris, son of Estphenis, and Berenebthis, son of Estphenis, and of Psintaes Junior, son of Estphenis, altogether 3 persons, to Milon, the attorney of Euphronios, praktor. Considering that it happened a) that you gave away our desertward field in Tkoitow and our nilewards field on Tmounpsobthis, which measures 30 aruras of field, against monetary payment, (and) b) that we have ransomed Variant: „ bought back“. it according to that which you have written in our presence According to Den Brinker / Muhs / Vleeming, DBL (2005), 109. Sethe, Bürgschaftsrecht (1920), Nr. 13: „vor uns“.; (and) c) that we already have delivered his first rate along with its interests; (and) d) that we already have paid them cash to the bank together with its interests and the remaining expenses relating to the value of the entire field; (and) e) that we do not have have enough money to pay you the other three instalments cash, as we are too weak of hand for it: So he should be given to Xenon, son of Dionysios, so that it is possible that he pays with cash the other three installments to the bank on their payment dates, so that nothing may disappear from the possession of Pharaoh. We will take possession of it (scil the field) again, after one has not let him (scil. Xenon) give an amount of money except for the silver coins together with their --?-- Issued by Estphenis on declaration of the people mentioned above, in the 25th regnal year, 30th of [Paophi]. Countersigned by Berenebthis, son of Estphenis. Countersigned by Pinyris, son of Estphenis. Countersigned by Psintaes, son of Estphenis. [///] This is the third memorandum of the priests mentioned above, concerning the land. 25th regnal year, 30th of Paophi.

translation_text: [en] [///] They will go to Isis in the 7th regnal year, [///]of Tybi. [///] here, to cause him his expense. May it be ordered by the lesonis concerning Thotomous, son of Petobastis, the sail maker Cf. Den Brinker / Muhs / Vleeming, DBL (2005), 54 for the reating variant „mast(?) maker“., and concerning the other craftsman being with him to cause them to come up. Moreover, the required work shall be made within 5 days. And it should be ordered concerning Pamounis, the master builder, to cause him to come up for cleaning the magazine. Thus has Harsiesis, son of Paes, this pastophor, spoken to me: ‘I have brought a letter to the lesonis.’ By Isis! I do not know anything about him. Nothing was said to me concerning this except for the statement of this pastophor. I have spoken to Payris, son of Petanoukis, the “great-of-the thousands” The tempus according to Den Brinker / Muhs / Vleeming, DBL (2005), 54., to cause him to come to you because of a palm tiller(?) required here. His replacement should be given to him, as soon as (the) palm tree arrives. When the palm tiller(?) will be given to him, I will send him i. e. Payris. to the lesonis on the very same time. If it happens that here is something which one will desire: One may order to send to me concerning it! Written by Pachnoumis, son of Horos, in the 7th regnal year, 8th of Tybi.

May it be ordered to allot it to Snebonychos in that (moment?) when he comes to you. Den Brinker / Muhs / Vleeming, DBL (2005), 54. by the overseer of the field! translation_text: [en] Harsiesis(?) [///] May it be ordered! Do not let him to be harmed! May he sprinkle water together with his colleague! May one look after him! That which will be done to him will be done to me(, too). Oh may Chnoum have 10 men “greats-of-the-thousands” who are also as servants with you! The temple of Elephantine is a great sanctuary. Do not let a conflict of men arise in it! If he takes offense, he shall be removed from it (scil. the sanctuary)! I will disburse his forfeit. I have not sent to the high priest concerning any matter again. If it happens that here is something (which is desired): May it be ordered to send to me concerning it! May it be ordered to act for me in agreement Reading variant Depauw, Dem. Letter 340, according to Vleeming, DBL in A. D. 2007 (2008). Reading variant Zauzich, P. Berl. Eleph. 2 (1993): „a transcript“. with this letter! Written by Psenyris(?) in the 2nd regnal year, 12th of Mesore.

If something is here, one may send to me concerning this! To [_] the lesonis.

translation_text: [en] Thus Hartophnachthes has spoken to me some things: ‘Eschnompmetis has sent to me concerning them.’ The sad matters brought [trouble] in your heart because I will not fulfill your instruction in everything, which will come. He Structure and translation of the following passages after Thesaurus Linguae Aegyptiae. But see Martin, Demotic Texts (1996), Nr. C9.(scil. Hartophnachthes) said the matters in question, after I have said the matters in question to Psammetichos, son of Pachnoumis to encourage your attorney with it. Behold, there are no (letters) which reached me before today. I would not have forgotten the like of these. One may send to me concerning that which is desired. Written in the 19th regnal year, 1st of Paophi. To [_] [Eschnompmetis].

translation_text: [en] (To) Eschnom(p)metis, son of Snebonychos, the lesonis of Chnoum. You have ordererd these letters to be brought. I have heard these [matters] concerning which you sent to me For translation cf. also Den Brinker / Muhs / Vleeming, DBL (2005).. You have sent on account of this 2 silver (deben) the following: Tiqi has caused me to give it. I have asked Tiqi. [_] He has told me: ‘It is you who applealed to me concerning this matter to give it Scil. the amount of money. to me.’ Cf. Den Brinker / Muhs / Vleeming, DBL (2005). Reading variant Zauzich, P. Berl. Eleph. 1 (1978): „… dass Du es bist, 6| den sie zu mir geschickt hat, um sie (die Geldbeträge) mir zu geben.“ That what I could do, I have done it. They Scil. the 2 deben. are redeemed among the amounts of money concerning which you have sent to me. Cf. Den Brinker / Muhs / Vleeming, DBL (2005). Reading variant Zauzich, P. Berl. Eleph. 1 (1978): „die Du mir geschickt hast.“ They have sent to Nechtminis, son of Sochotes, who inspects in the temple of Elephantine. Written by Belles in the 18th regnal year, 28th Thoth. To Eschnompmetis [_] son of Snebonychos, [the] lesonis of Chnoum.

translation_text: [en] Voice of Horos, son of Kolluthos before Spotous, Chnoummachis (and) Osor[oeris], the wab priests of Haroeris. Oh may Pre cause their lifetime to be long! The 17th of Phamenoth happened, and I came to Philae with Komoapis, son of Parates, (with) Patus, son of Pachois, (and with) Panouphis, son of Komoapis, to worship you (and) to visit you. We are told: ‘They traveled south to the land of the Nubians.’ Did you not realize, that we worry about the (situation) in which you are much more than you (by yourself?)? We fear Chnoum even more than you! Every calamity which we received on the voyage, it was on account of the matters in question, in order to keep our gods safe. Once this letter reaches you, come (back) to your temple! Your breath: May it be thriving! Do not stop coming to the Abaton in the night! Do not let the god penalize For translation, cf. Den Brinker / Muhs / Vleeming, DBL (2005). me in some manner! I have queried Nepherpres, the priest of Chnoum, about the well-being of the temples. There is no disadvantage. I came to Syene when it was evening of the 15th day. I finished the matters in Syene on the 16th day. I have not lost time to come and to meet with you, rising early to come to Philae today and to meet with you, because it was close to my heart. (But) you consider us to be enemies! Our heart is more open towards the god than towards you. Once you see this letter, do not delay to come (back) to your temple! Written by Horos in the 18th regnal year, 17th of Phamenoth. To [_] the wab priests of Chnoum.

translation_text: [en] [I have sent a letter to you earlier to cause you to ask about my well-being before Isis, and about that of (my) children and about the journeys which I will undertake again. You have not sent to me concerning anything to which you gave attention For the reading cf. Den Brinker / Muhs / Vleeming, DBL (2005), 51. Reading variant Zauzich, P. Berl. Eleph. 1 (1978): „die Du deswegen unternommen hast“.. Let it be ordered to cause one to ask about my well-being (still) today, and (about) the well-being of my wife and (my) children, and (about) the well-being of my own place and the matters which I told you (and) which affect this man. Written by Peteesis, son of Pete-[///], in the 3rd regnal year, 30th of Paophi.

translation_text: [en] [///] They will go to Isis in the 7th regnal year, [///]of Tybi. [///] here, to cause him his expense. May it be ordered by the lesonis concerning Thotomous, son of Petobastis, the sail maker Cf. Den Brinker / Muhs / Vleeming, DBL (2005), 54 for the reating variant „mast(?) maker“., and concerning the other craftsman being with him to cause them to come up. Moreover, the required work shall be made within 5 days. And it should be ordered concerning Pamounis, the master builder, to cause him to come up for cleaning the magazine. Thus has Harsiesis, son of Paes, this pastophor, spoken to me: ‘I have brought a letter to the lesonis.’ By Isis! I do not know anything about him. Nothing was said to me concerning this except for the statement of this pastophor. I have spoken to Payris, son of Petanoukis, the “great-of-the thousands” The tempus according to Den Brinker / Muhs / Vleeming, DBL (2005), 54., to cause him to come to you because of a palm tiller(?) required here. His replacement should be given to him, as soon as (the) palm tree arrives. When the palm tiller(?) will be given to him, I will send him i. e. Payris. to the lesonis on the very same time. If it happens that here is something which one will desire: One may order to send to me concerning it! Written by Pachnoumis, son of Horos, in the 7th regnal year, 8th of Tybi.

Den Brinker / Muhs / Vleeming, DBL (2005), 25. nty ḥry sẖ Ḥr-pa-ꜣs.t In line 1 and 14 the name of this person is given as Harpaesis. sꜣ Pꜣ-wp.ṱ ḏd ı͗w=y (r) ı͗r r-ẖ mḏ(.t) nb nty sẖ ḥry n nꜣ ssw.w nty ḥry 2nd copy Ḥr-pa-ꜣs.t sꜣ Pꜣ-wp.ṱ Ns-pꜣ-mtr sꜣ Ḏḥwtı͗-mꜣꜥ Pa-rṱ sꜣ Pa-ḥr r s 3 nꜣ nty ḏd (n) wꜥ rꜣ tw=n
translation_text: [en] Harpaesis, son of Pouoptis, Espmetis, son of Thotomous, (and) Parates, son of Payris – yields 3 people – are the one who say unanimously: We take an oath before Pharaoh Ptolemaios (III.), son of Ptolemaios (II.) and Arsinoë (II.) Philadelpha {and} the gods Adelphoi, the gods Euergetai and the Female Pharaoh Berenike (II.), his sister, his wife {and the gods Adelphoi, the gods Euergetai} and Isis and Osiris and all the gods of Egypt. We will collect the amounts of money of the salt tax and the amounts of money of the wool tax of the district of Elephantine, for the tax of the 11th regnal year, the 3 people in accordance with that which will be accounted for us in the houses of writing of Pharaoh, without any men saying something wrong to his companion among us, the 3 people. And they shall be paid to the banks of Pharaoh until we have paid them in full. And we will bear the allowance and the loss between us, the 3 people. That which shall arise as profit, we will divide it among us, the 3 people, so that there is no falsehood n the oath. And we will write the money which shall be paid to us, the 3 people, `and´ the expense which shall arise therein, `in a papyrus roll and we will seal it´. The one who will withdraw from it according with everything mentioned above, he should pay 5 silver (deben) (to) his companion among us. Issued by Parates, son of Payris, on declaration of the three men, in the 11th regnal year, Payni of Pharaoh Ptolemaios (III.) LPH, son of Ptolemaios (II.) LPH. [_] Countersigned by Espmetis, son of Thotomous, to act in accordance with everything mentioned above, in the times mentioned above. [_] Countersigned by Harsiesis, son of Pouoptis: I will act in accordance with everything written above, in the times mentioned above. Harpaesis, son of Pouoptis, Espmetis, son of Thotomous, (and) Parates, son of Payris – yields 3 people – are the one who say unanimously: ⸢We⸣ [///]
